insight: For Brazil, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.100 minutes per break, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when Brazil is not pressing before a break, they are able to regain some momentum afterwards, as seen in the one instance where they had a positive change of +0.100 minutes per minute, whereas when they were pressing before a break, they lost more momentum, with a change of -0.300 minutes per minute, and were unable to recover it. This implies that Brazil may need to adjust their strategy when going into a hydration break, particularly if they are pressing, in order to maintain their momentum and performance.
